keyPointsSummary: 'Might have' is even more uncertain than 'may have' about a past event
Often used in hypothetical or very tentative statements

quickQuiz.hindi: वह शायद गया होगा, लेकिन पक्का नहीं।
quickQuiz.answer: He might have gone, but I am not sure.

quickQuiz.hindi: शायद उन्होंने मेरी बात मानी होगी।
quickQuiz.answer: They might have agreed with me.

quickQuiz.hindi: शायद वह वहाँ पहुँच गया होगा।
शायद उसने मुझे भूल दिया होगा।
शायद उन्होंने यह प्लान बदल दिया होगा।
quickQuiz.answer: They might have changed this plan.

quickQuiz.hindi: शायद वह इसके बारे में जानता होगा।
quickQuiz.answer: He might have known about this.

quickQuiz.hindi: शायद उसने यह नहीं देखा होगा।
quickQuiz.answer: She might not have seen this.

quickQuiz.hindi: शायद हम गलत रास्ते पर चले गए होंगे।
quickQuiz.answer: We might have gone the wrong way.

mustRememberRules: 'Might have' is even more uncertain than 'may have' about a past event
Often used in hypothetical or very tentative statements
